meta.title: Lucas Alves | Dasial | Backend Software Engineer
meta.description: Lucas Alves, Backend Software Engineer specializing in Elixir, Phoenix, PostgreSQL and AWS. Building scalable backend systems for digital banking, employee benefits, fleet management and local government platforms.
hero.lead: Developing solutions for real-world problems for over +5 years through services and apps that make life easier for thousands of people and businesses every day.
hero.lead2: I believe great software isn't defined by the programming language it's built with, but by its ability to solve problems in a simple, reliable, and sustainable way.
aboutpage.title: Lucas Alves | About
aboutpage.description: About Lucas Alves, Backend Software Engineer: background, skills and technologies across backend, mobile and cloud.
about.title: About
about.alias: Also known as Dasial, short for Da(Silva) Al(ves).
about.p1: I started my career as a Full Stack Developer working with Vue.js and Node.js, then spent two years building mobile applications with React Native and Flutter. After working across the stack, I intentionally transitioned into Backend Engineering to go deeper into software architecture, APIs, and robust systems.
about.p2: Today I work primarily with Elixir, Phoenix, and PostgreSQL, building backend services for digital banking, employee benefits, fleet management, and local government platforms used by thousands of people every day. My day-to-day includes designing RESTful APIs, integrating financial and third-party platforms, optimizing database performance, and running asynchronous workloads with Oban.
about.p3: I enjoy solving complex engineering problems and I'm always looking to strengthen my technical foundations. With each challenge, I try to understand the client's pain point and the context of the problem, deepening my knowledge in the field to develop efficient, high-impact technical solutions.
about.values.title: What I value
about.growth.title: Currently deepening
skills.title: Skills
experience.title: Experience
exp1.summary: Transitioned from Mobile Engineering to Backend Engineering after gaining hands-on production experience with Elixir in a second role. I now focus on designing and building backend services and financial applications that support thousands of users and process high volumes of daily transactions.
exp1.b1: Deliver RESTful APIs in Elixir and Phoenix powering banking, benefits, fleet, and local government platforms used by thousands of people daily.
exp1.b2: Sustain high daily transaction volumes without compromising reliability by architecting business-critical backend features for scale.
exp1.b3: Improve system responsiveness and reduce processing bottlenecks by implementing asynchronous background jobs with Oban.
exp1.b4: Boost API performance by optimizing PostgreSQL queries and streamlining integrations with internal services and third-party financial platforms.
exp1.b5: Accelerate the development cycle by automating recurring workflows with Shell Scripts and Docker.
exp1.b6: Drive end-to-end feature delivery by partnering closely with Product Managers, Designers, and Engineers across the full software development lifecycle.
exp2.summary1: This was the experience that shaped my consolidation as a software engineer. It's where I matured professionally, developing software with a focus on quality, scalability, and business impact.
exp2.summary2: I worked in an environment with high engineering standards, following development best practices, agile methodologies, and close collaboration with multidisciplinary teams. It was also at this company that I began my journey in remote work, strengthening my autonomy, communication, and accountability in delivery.
exp2.b1: Spearheaded mobile development from day one of a digital banking platform, shaping its technical foundation as lead developer.
exp2.b2: Modernized the mobile stack by leading the migration from React Native to Flutter across multiple production applications, improving long-term maintainability.
exp2.b3: Strengthened code maintainability by introducing MVVM architecture and dependency injection across the mobile codebase.
exp2.b4: Scaled a single codebase into more than 30 branded, white-label apps, multiplying product reach without duplicating engineering effort.
exp2.b5: Enabled seamless data flow across platforms by integrating cross-platform applications with REST and GraphQL APIs.
exp2.b6: Raised team-wide technical capability by delivering internal presentations that shared knowledge across the engineering team.
exp2.project: StockPulse: an app I helped build from the idea stage to production
exp2.project2: Franco: the digital banking platform I led as mobile developer
exp3.summary: This is where my journey as a software engineer began: my first professional role, where I built full-stack web applications and laid the foundation that led me toward Backend Engineering.
exp3.project: My first professional project: Leal Sistemas institutional website
exp3.b1: Built and shipped web applications end-to-end using Vue.js, Node.js, and Firebase, from backend services to customer-facing UI.
exp3.b2: Strengthened code reliability across the team by introducing TypeScript into the development workflow.
exp3.b3: Expanded product capability by delivering customer-facing features alongside internal tools that supported day-to-day operations.
exp3.b4: Bridged frontend and backend development to ship cohesive, full-stack features across the product.
